qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GIF addImage parsing permits crafted dimensions to crash the Node process
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a node-build local file inclusion/path traversal in the loadFile-based file-reading path, while this finding is an availability failure in GIF decoding after image bytes are already supplied to addImage.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding is a reproduced GIF parser allocation-failure DoS. The advisory instead describes unrestricted local file reads in the node-only loadFile path and in APIs that route attacker-controlled path strings into that file-reading helper.
evidence: The advisory summary and upstream fix target node-build file access in src/modules/fileloading.js, where v3.0.4 resolves a caller-controlled path and reads it with fs.readFileSync(path.resolve(url)); commit a688c8f479929b24a6543b1fa2d6364abb03066d adds allowFsRead and Node permission checks to that path.

In src/modules/addimage.js, addImage reaches the advisory-relevant file-reading branch only when string input is not recognized as inline image data and the code falls back to jsPDFAPI.loadFile(imageData, true).

The accepted finding uses crafted GIF bytes and reaches src/modules/gif_support.js processGIF89A and src/libs/omggif.js decodeAndBlitFrameRGBA, where frame.width * frame.height drives new Uint8Array(num_pixels) and causes an allocation failure. That is a different source-to-sink path and a different root cause from unrestricted local file reads.
